title: Podman CLI fails to set proper network alias across multiple networks [flaky but easy to reproduce]
body:
### Issue Description

When using multiple networks, podman fails to set network aliases properly in them. This causes the connectivity issues.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue

1. `git clone https://github.com/cardil/repro-podman-netalias.git && cd repro-podman-netalias`
2. `podman-compose up --abort-on-container-exit`

The repo contains a simple `compose.yaml` with 3 services: `redis`, `app`, `data` where `redis` and `app` are in `apps` network, and `data` and `redis` are in `data` network.

```
                                              data
                     ┌────────────────────────────────────┐
    apps             │                                    │
┌────────────────────┼──────────────────┐                 │
│                    │                  │                 │
│  ┌────────────┐    │  ┌────────────┐  │  ┌────────────┐ │
│  │            │    │  │            │  │  │            │ │
│  │    app     ├────┼─►│   redis    │◄─┼──┤    data    │ │
│  │            │    │  │            │  │  │            │ │
│  └────────────┘    │  └────────────┘  │  └────────────┘ │
│                    │                  │                 │
└────────────────────┼──────────────────┘                 │
                     │                                    │
                     └────────────────────────────────────┘
```

The same outcome can be reproduced manually, buiding images and calling podman commands that podman-compose generates.

When running `docker-compose` on Podman socket, the issue isn't present, so I think it must be located somewhere in CLI layer.

### Describe the results you received

See the execution log: https://gist.github.com/cardil/2077037d3c804fc96d956137bffeef65

The app fails to boot because it cannot connect to the Redis database (via `redis` name). The app has 2 networks. Running with just one network is a workaround.

Sometimes this actually works as expected, but it's rare. It works well, when running via docker-compose and podman socket.

### Describe the results you expected

I expected the connectivity to always work in specified networks.

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: I tried reproducing on Gcloud, on Fedora 38, but couldn't - it works there. The `podman info` shows the Gcloud Fedora runs on `aardvark-dns` driver, as opposed to my PC running `cni`.

We recommend the use of netavark/aardvark-dns.  CNI network is deprecated.

You have to reset your storage on your laptop in order to switch to netavark

Yes, I found the instruction, here: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/3e86bece98e7a203e62162ac0ddbe52287875bce/docs/tutorials/basic_networking.md?plain=1#L85-L87

I think it should be communicated in some way... podman didn't given me any message about deprecation, that's why I posted this issue.
